title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder] (https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion). answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to geobounds
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to geobounds
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to geobounds
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("dieghernan/geobounds", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to geobounds
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to geobounds
text: Please note geobounds project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: geobounds: Download administrative boundaries in R
text: geobounds package provides interface downloading working administrative boundaries geoBoundaries Global Database Political Administrative Boundaries (Runfola et al. 2020). default gbOpen release type covers countries worldwide across multiple ADM levels. boundaries use multiple open licenses, including ODbL, CC CC -SA. package also supports gbHumanitarian gbAuthoritative, differ sources, validation processes licensing. geobounds, can download boundaries sf objects, inspect boundary metadata, cache downloaded files integrate boundaries spatial workflows. vignette keeps main workflow one place. first explains choose right boundary type, covers cache management finishes spatial analysis example.

title: geobounds: Download administrative boundaries in R
text: geoBoundaries database designed scientific academic use, quality assurance includes manual review hand digitization physical maps necessary. precision comes cost: files can quite large may take longer download. visualization general mapping purposes, recommend using simplified boundaries setting simplified = TRUE. Comparison full-resolution simplified boundaries. geoBoundaries API provides individual country boundaries reflect countries represent boundaries, without special identification disputed areas. Download individual country boundaries gb_get() gb_get_adm*() wrappers. Borders guaranteed align perfectly, gaps may exist countries disputed territories may represented consistently. Map showing overlap disputed Kashmir area. individual country boundary governed license identified boundary metadata. sharing boundary derived product, attribute geoBoundaries original providers shown metadata. Include applicable license, link terms indicate modifications required. ODbL CC -SA data may impose share-alike obligations beyond attribution. Use gb_get_world() global composite boundaries standardize disputed areas fill gaps borders. boundaries also known Comprehensive Global Administrative Zones (CGAZ). differ individual country boundaries three important ways: Extensive simplification keeps file sizes small enough desktop software. Disputed areas removed replaced polygons following United States Department State definitions. Gaps borders filled. CGAZ boundaries figures covered package’s MIT license. Follow citation use information included downloaded CGAZ archive. Map showing overlap Kashmir, provided CGAZ.
code: library(geobounds) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  # Compare resolutions. norway <- gb_get_adm0("NOR") |>   mutate(res = "Full resolution") print(object.size(norway), units = "Mb") #> 26.5 Mb  norway_simp <- gb_get_adm0(country = "NOR", simplified = TRUE) |>   mutate(res = "Simplified") print(object.size(norway_simp), units = "Mb") #> 1.5 Mb  norway_all <- bind_rows(norway, norway_simp)  # Plot with ggplot2. ggplot(norway_all) +   geom_sf(fill = "#BA0C2F", color = "#00205B") +   facet_wrap(vars(res)) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     caption = paste(       "Sources: geoBoundaries and the original boundary provider,",       "check gb_get_metadata() for the license"     )   ) india_pak <- gb_get_adm0(c("India", "Pakistan"))  # Highlight the disputed Kashmir area. ggplot(india_pak) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = shapeName), alpha = 0.5) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF671F", "#00401A")) +   labs(     fill = "Country",     title = "Map of India and Pakistan",     subtitle = "Note the overlap in the Kashmir region",     caption = paste(       "Sources: geoBoundaries and the original boundary providers,",       "check gb_get_metadata() for licenses"     )   ) gb_get_metadata(c("India", "Pakistan"), adm_lvl = "ADM0") |>   select(     boundaryName,     boundaryLicense,     boundarySource,     licenseSource   ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   boundaryName boundaryLicense                                      boundarySource licenseSource #>   <chr>        <chr>                                                <chr>          <chr>         #> 1 India        CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication geoBoundaries… commons.wiki… #> 2 Pakistan     Open Data Commons Open Database License 1.0          OpenStreetMap… www.openstre… cgaz_india_pak <- gb_get_world(c("India", "Pakistan"))  ggplot(cgaz_india_pak) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = shapeName), alpha = 0.5) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF671F", "#00401A")) +   labs(     fill = "Country",     title = "Map of India and Pakistan",     subtitle = "CGAZ does not overlap",     caption = "Source: geoBoundaries (CGAZ)"   )

title: geobounds: Download administrative boundaries in R
text: geoBoundaries API provides individual country boundaries reflect countries represent boundaries, without special identification disputed areas. Download individual country boundaries gb_get() gb_get_adm*() wrappers. Borders guaranteed align perfectly, gaps may exist countries disputed territories may represented consistently. Map showing overlap disputed Kashmir area. individual country boundary governed license identified boundary metadata. sharing boundary derived product, attribute geoBoundaries original providers shown metadata. Include applicable license, link terms indicate modifications required. ODbL CC -SA data may impose share-alike obligations beyond attribution.
code: india_pak <- gb_get_adm0(c("India", "Pakistan"))  # Highlight the disputed Kashmir area. ggplot(india_pak) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = shapeName), alpha = 0.5) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF671F", "#00401A")) +   labs(     fill = "Country",     title = "Map of India and Pakistan",     subtitle = "Note the overlap in the Kashmir region",     caption = paste(       "Sources: geoBoundaries and the original boundary providers,",       "check gb_get_metadata() for licenses"     )   ) gb_get_metadata(c("India", "Pakistan"), adm_lvl = "ADM0") |>   select(     boundaryName,     boundaryLicense,     boundarySource,     licenseSource   ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   boundaryName boundaryLicense                                      boundarySource licenseSource #>   <chr>        <chr>                                                <chr>          <chr>         #> 1 India        CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication geoBoundaries… commons.wiki… #> 2 Pakistan     Open Data Commons Open Database License 1.0          OpenStreetMap… www.openstre…

text: Use gb_get_world() global composite boundaries standardize disputed areas fill gaps borders. boundaries also known Comprehensive Global Administrative Zones (CGAZ). differ individual country boundaries three important ways: Extensive simplification keeps file sizes small enough desktop software. Disputed areas removed replaced polygons following United States Department State definitions. Gaps borders filled. CGAZ boundaries figures covered package’s MIT license. Follow citation use information included downloaded CGAZ archive. Map showing overlap Kashmir, provided CGAZ.

title: geobounds: Download administrative boundaries in R
text: geobounds stores downloaded files cache directory repeated requests country ADM level can reuse cached file. example: clear cache, use gb_clear_cache(). Use cache_dir argument set cache directory individual function call.
code: # Show the current cache directory. current <- gb_detect_cache_dir() #> ℹ 'C:\Users\RUNNER~1\AppData\Local\Temp\Rtmp6LfQDv'  current #> [1] "C:\\Users\\RUNNER~1\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\Rtmp6LfQDv"  # Change to a new cache directory. newdir <- file.path(tempdir(), "/geoboundvignette") gb_set_cache_dir(newdir) #> ✔ geobounds cache directory is 'C:\Users\RUNNER~1\AppData\Local\Temp\Rtmp6LfQDv//geoboundvignette'. #> ℹ To use this cache directory in future sessions, call `gb_set_cache_dir()` with `install = TRUE`.  # Download the example data. example <- gb_get_adm0("Vatican City", quiet = FALSE) #> ℹ Downloading file from <https://github.com/wmgeolab/geoBoundaries/raw/9469f09/releaseData/gbOpen/VAT/ADM0/geoBoundaries-VAT-ADM0-all.zip>. #> → Cache directory is 'C:\Users\RUNNER~1\AppData\Local\Temp\Rtmp6LfQDv//geoboundvignette/gbOpen'.  # Restore the cache directory. gb_set_cache_dir(current) #> ✔ geobounds cache directory is 'C:\Users\RUNNER~1\AppData\Local\Temp\Rtmp6LfQDv'. #> ℹ To use this cache directory in future sessions, call `gb_set_cache_dir()` with `install = TRUE`.  current == gb_detect_cache_dir() #> ℹ 'C:\Users\RUNNER~1\AppData\Local\Temp\Rtmp6LfQDv' #> [1] TRUE

title: geobounds: Download administrative boundaries in R
text: boundaries returned sf objects, can combine spatial data: Clip raster data administrative units. Compute zonal statistics. Create choropleth maps. Perform spatial joins survey tabular data. example creates choropleth map using metadata individual country boundaries global composite boundaries CGAZ: World Bank Income Group: Latin America Caribbean.
code: # Get boundary metadata. latam_meta <- gb_get_metadata(adm_lvl = "ADM0") |>   select(boundaryISO, boundaryName, Continent, worldBankIncomeGroup) |>   filter(Continent == "Latin America and the Caribbean") |>   glimpse() #> Rows: 47 #> Columns: 4 #> $ boundaryISO          <chr> "ABW", "AIA", "ARG", "ATG", "BES", "BHS", "BLM", "BLZ", "BOL", "B… #> $ boundaryName         <chr> "Aruba", "Anguilla", "Argentina", "Antigua and Barbuda", "Bonaire… #> $ Continent            <chr> "Latin America and the Caribbean", "Latin America and the Caribbe… #> $ worldBankIncomeGroup <chr> "High-income Countries", "No income group available", "High-incom…  # Adjust factors. latam_meta$income_factor <- factor(   latam_meta$worldBankIncomeGroup,   levels = c(     "High-income Countries",     "Upper-middle-income Countries",     "Lower-middle-income Countries",     "Low-income Countries"   ) )  # Get global composite boundaries from CGAZ. latam_sf <- gb_get_world(adm_lvl = "ADM0") |>   inner_join(latam_meta, by = c("shapeGroup" = "boundaryISO"))  ggplot(latam_sf) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = income_factor)) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Greens", direction = -1) +   guides(fill = guide_legend(position = "bottom", nrow = 2)) +   coord_sf(     crs = "+proj=laea +lon_0=-75 +lat_0=-15"   ) +   labs(     title = "World Bank Income Group",     subtitle = "Latin America and the Caribbean",     fill = "",     caption = "Source: geoBoundaries (CGAZ and gbOpen metadata)"   )

Returns boundaries as sf objects for mapping and spatial analysis. Runfola et al. (2020) <doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0231866> describe the underlying database.},   doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.geobounds}, } @Article{10.1371/journal.pone.0231866,   title = {{geoBoundaries}: A global database of political administrative boundaries},   author = {Daniel Runfola and Austin Anderson and Heather Baier and Matt Crittenden and Elizabeth Dowker and Sydney Fuhrig and Seth Goodman and Grace Grimsley and Rachel Layko and Graham Melville and Maddy Mulder and Rachel Oberman and Joshua Panganiban and Andrew Peck and Leigh Seitz and Sylvia Shea and Hannah Slevin and Rebecca Youngerman and Lauren Hobbs},   year = {2020},   month = {apr},   journal = {PLOS ONE},   publisher = {Public Library of Science},   volume = {15},   number = {4},   pages = {1--9},   doi = {10.1371/journal.pone.0231866},   url = {https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0231866}, }

title: geobounds | Download administrative boundaries from geoBoundaries
text: geobounds package provides R-friendly interface downloading administrative boundaries geoBoundaries, Global Database Political Administrative Boundaries. geobounds, can: Download individual country boundaries global composite boundaries geoBoundaries. Use tidyverse sf workflows R map, analyze join administrative boundaries data. Work open-data context attribution geoBoundaries required. short, work administrative boundaries R, geobounds simplifies downloading, cache management integration spatial data workflows. Attribution required sharing geoBoundaries boundaries derived products.

title: geobounds | Download administrative boundaries from geoBoundaries
text: Install geobounds CRAN:
code: install.packages("geobounds")

title: geobounds | Download administrative boundaries from geoBoundaries
code: library(geobounds)  sri_lanka_adm1 <- gb_get_adm1("Sri Lanka") sri_lanka_adm2 <- gb_get_adm2("Sri Lanka") sri_lanka_adm3 <- gb_get_adm3("Sri Lanka")  library(sf) library(dplyr)  library(ggplot2)  ggplot(sri_lanka_adm3) +   geom_sf(fill = "#DFDFDF", color = "white") +   geom_sf(data = sri_lanka_adm2, fill = NA, color = "#F0B323") +   geom_sf(data = sri_lanka_adm1, fill = NA, color = "black") +   labs(     caption = paste(       "Sources: geoBoundaries, OpenStreetMap and Wambacher,",       "license: ODbL 1.0"     )   ) +   theme_void()

title: geobounds | Download administrative boundaries from geoBoundaries
text: geoBoundaries provides three release types different data sources, validation processes licensing terms: gbOpen: Openly licensed boundaries suitable uses. original license varies boundary may include ODbL, CC CC -SA terms. gbHumanitarian: Boundaries mirrored UN OCHA. Check current metadata applicable source, license attribution terms. gbAuthoritative: Boundaries mirrored UN SALB verified -country processes. boundaries used commercial purposes. Use release_type argument request specific release type, example, gb_get_adm1("Sri Lanka", release_type = "gbHumanitarian"). coverage boundary metadata release type, see geobounds articles.

title: geobounds | Download administrative boundaries from geoBoundaries
text: Map highest available ADM level geoBoundaries country:
code: library(geobounds) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  world <- gb_get_world() max_lvl <- gb_get_max_adm_lvl(release_type = "gbOpen")  world_max <- world |>   mutate(boundaryISO = shapeGroup) |>   left_join(max_lvl) |>   mutate(max_lvl = factor(maxBoundaryType, levels = 0:5))  pal <- c("#0e221b", "#0f4a38", "#0b6e4f", "#719384", "#b9975a", "#936e28") names(pal) <- levels(world_max$max_lvl)  ggplot(world_max) +   geom_sf(fill = "#e5e5e5", color = "#e5e5e5") +   geom_sf(aes(fill = max_lvl), color = "transparent") +   scale_fill_manual(values = pal, na.translate = FALSE, drop = FALSE) +   guides(fill = guide_legend(direction = "horizontal", nrow = 1)) +   coord_sf(expand = TRUE, crs = "+proj=robin") +   theme_void() +   theme(     plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),     text = element_text(family = "sans", face = "bold"),     legend.position = "bottom",     legend.title.position = "top",     legend.title = element_text(size = rel(0.75), face = "plain"),     legend.text = element_text(size = rel(1)),     legend.text.position = "right",     legend.key.height = unit(1, "line"),     legend.key.width = unit(1, "line"),     plot.caption = element_text(       size = rel(0.7),       margin = margin(r = 4)     )   ) +   labs(     fill = "gbOpen: Highest available ADM level",     caption = "Source: geoBoundaries (CGAZ and gbOpen metadata)"   )

what: Clear the geobounds cache directory — gb_clear_cache
title: Clear the geobounds cache directory — gb_clear_cache
text: Use function caution. clears cached archives configuration deleting geobounds configuration directory (tools::R_user_dir("geobounds", "config")), deleting active cache directory clearing Sys.getenv("GEOBOUNDS_CACHE_DIR").

title: Clear the geobounds cache directory — gb_clear_cache
code: gb_clear_cache(config = FALSE, cached_data = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)

title: Clear the geobounds cache directory — gb_clear_cache
text: config logical value. TRUE, delete geobounds configuration directory. cached_data logical value. TRUE, delete active cache directory contents. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppress informational messages.

title: Clear the geobounds cache directory — gb_clear_cache
text: Invisibly returns NULL. function called side effects.

title: Clear the geobounds cache directory — gb_clear_cache
text: reset restores cache state fresh geobounds installation.

title: Clear the geobounds cache directory — gb_clear_cache
code: # Caution: this may modify your current state.  # \dontrun{ my_cache <- gb_detect_cache_dir() #> ℹ /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds # Set an example cache directory. ex <- file.path(tempdir(), "example", "cache") gb_set_cache_dir(ex, quiet = TRUE)  gb_clear_cache(quiet = FALSE) #> ! Deleted the geobounds cache directory /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/example/cache.  # Restore the initial cache. gb_set_cache_dir(my_cache) #> ✔ geobounds cache directory is /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds. #> ℹ To use this cache directory in future sessions, call `gb_set_cache_dir()` with `install = TRUE`. identical(my_cache, gb_detect_cache_dir()) #> ℹ /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds #> [1] TRUE # }

what: Detect the geobounds cache directory — gb_detect_cache_dir
title: Detect the geobounds cache directory — gb_detect_cache_dir
text: Detects active cache directory. See gb_set_cache_dir().

title: Detect the geobounds cache directory — gb_detect_cache_dir
code: gb_detect_cache_dir(x = NULL)

title: Detect the geobounds cache directory — gb_detect_cache_dir
text: x object. Ignored.

title: Detect the geobounds cache directory — gb_detect_cache_dir
text: character scalar containing path active cache directory. path also printed clickable message. See cli::inline-markup cli.

title: Detect the geobounds cache directory — gb_detect_cache_dir
code: gb_detect_cache_dir() #> ℹ /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds"

what: Download individual country boundaries from geoBoundaries — gb_get
title: Download individual country boundaries from geoBoundaries — gb_get
text: Returns individual country boundaries reflect countries represent boundaries, without special identification disputed areas. Use gb_get_world() global composite boundaries standardize disputed areas fill gaps borders. Boundaries downloaded function covered package's MIT license. Attribution geoBoundaries original sources required sharing boundaries derived products.

title: Download individual country boundaries from geoBoundaries — gb_get
code: gb_get(   country,   adm_lvl = "adm0",   simplified = FALSE,   release_type = c("gbOpen", "gbHumanitarian", "gbAuthoritative"),   quiet = TRUE,   overwrite = FALSE,   cache_dir = NULL )

title: Download individual country boundaries from geoBoundaries — gb_get
text: geoBoundaries API.

title: Download individual country boundaries from geoBoundaries — gb_get
text: country character vector country names ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. Use "" return boundaries countries. See also countrycode::countrycode() countrycode. adm_lvl ADM level. Accepted values "" (available boundaries) ADM level ("adm0" country boundary, "adm1" first level subnational boundaries, "adm2" second level ). Uppercase versions ("ADM1") level numbers (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) also accepted. simplified logical value. TRUE, return simplified boundaries. default FALSE uses primary geoBoundaries layer. See simplified boundaries https://www.geoboundaries.org/. release_type character string, one "gbOpen", "gbHumanitarian" "gbAuthoritative". users, use "gbOpen" (default), contains openly licensed boundaries suitable purposes individual license terms followed. "gbHumanitarian" boundaries mirrored UN OCHA may additional conditions. "gbAuthoritative" boundaries mirrored UN SALB, verified -country processes used commercial purposes. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppress informational messages. overwrite logical value. TRUE, force fresh download source .zip archive. cache_dir path cache directory. set (default NULL), boundary archives stored default cache directory (see gb_set_cache_dir()). cache directory set, archives stored temporary cache directory. See base::tempdir() cache strategies gb_set_cache_dir().

title: Download individual country boundaries from geoBoundaries — gb_get
text: sf object sf containing requested boundaries. boundaries match request, function returns NULL.

title: Download individual country boundaries from geoBoundaries — gb_get
text: individual country boundary layer governed original license identified boundary metadata. See gb_get_metadata(). "gbOpen" release contains multiple open licenses, including ODbL CC -SA. assume every boundary licensed CC 4.0. Users cite sources listed metadata comply attribution, share-alike non-commercial terms. wrappers gb_get_adm0(), gb_get_adm1(), gb_get_adm2(), gb_get_adm3(), gb_get_adm4() gb_get_adm5() also available requesting single ADM level.

A tibble: 25 × 6 #>    shapeName     shapeISO shapeID shapeGroup shapeType                  geometry #>  * <chr>         <chr>    <chr>   <chr>      <chr>            <MULTIPOLYGON [°]> #>  1 Jaffna Distr… LK-41    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((79.7152 9.529465, 79.… #>  2 Kilinochchi … LK-42    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((80.01015 9.472403, 80… #>  3 Mannar Distr… LK-43    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((80.11535 9.209068, 80… #>  4 Mullaitivu D… LK-45    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((80.61353 9.456581, 80… #>  5 Vavuniya Dis… LK-44    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((80.23541 8.680412, 80… #>  6 Galle Distri… LK-31    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((79.98757 6.440352, 79… #>  7 Hambantota D… LK-33    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((80.67006 6.306029, 80… #>  8 Matara Distr… LK-32    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((80.3818 5.965264, 80.… #>  9 Ampara Distr… LK-52    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((81.70788 6.51073, 81.… #> 10 Anuradhapura… LK-71    463711… LKA        ADM2      (((80.03237 8.527211, 80… #> # ℹ 15 more rows  library(ggplot2) ggplot(sri_lanka) +   geom_sf() +   labs(     caption = paste(       "Sources: geoBoundaries, OpenStreetMap and Wambacher,",       "license: ODbL 1.0"     )   )  # }  # Inspect boundary metadata. library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union gb_get_metadata(   "Sri Lanka",   adm_lvl = 2 ) |>   # what: Download country boundaries for one ADM level — gb_get_adm
title: Download country boundaries for one ADM level — gb_get_adm
text: functions call gb_get() single ADM level. gb_get_adm0() returns country boundaries, gb_get_adm1() returns first-level subnational boundaries (example, states United States) gb_get_adm2() returns second-level subnational boundaries (example, counties United States). gb_get_adm3(), gb_get_adm4() gb_get_adm5() return third-, fourth- fifth-level administrative boundaries, respectively. countries number ADM levels. Use gb_get_max_adm_lvl() check availability. Boundaries downloaded functions covered package's MIT license. Attribution geoBoundaries original sources required sharing boundaries derived products.

title: Download country boundaries for one ADM level — gb_get_adm

title: Retrieve boundary metadata from geoBoundaries — gb_get_metadata
text: country character vector country names ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. Use "" return boundaries countries. See also countrycode::countrycode() countrycode. adm_lvl ADM level. Accepted values "" (available boundaries) ADM level ("adm0" country boundary, "adm1" first level subnational boundaries, "adm2" second level ). Uppercase versions ("ADM1") level numbers (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) also accepted. release_type character string, one "gbOpen", "gbHumanitarian" "gbAuthoritative". users, use "gbOpen" (default), contains openly licensed boundaries suitable purposes individual license terms followed. "gbHumanitarian" boundaries mirrored UN OCHA may additional conditions. "gbAuthoritative" boundaries mirrored UN SALB, verified -country processes used commercial purposes.

title: Retrieve boundary metadata from geoBoundaries — gb_get_metadata
text: tibble tibble one row per matching boundary columns described Details.

title: Retrieve boundary metadata from geoBoundaries — gb_get_metadata
text: result tibble tibble following columns: boundaryID: ID layer. combines ISO code, boundary type unique identifier generated input metadata geometry. changes underlying boundary changes. boundaryName: name country represented layer. boundaryISO: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code country. boundaryYearRepresented: year range years "START END" format boundary layers represent. boundaryType: type boundary. boundaryCanonical: canonical name boundary. boundarySource: comma-separated list primary sources boundary. boundaryLicense: original license primary source released boundary. licenseDetail: Notes license. licenseSource: URL primary source. sourceDataUpdateDate: date source information integrated geoBoundaries repository. buildDate: date source boundary recently standardized built geoBoundaries release. Continent: continent country associated . UNSDG-region: United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) region country associated . UNSDG-subregion: United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) subregion country associated . worldBankIncomeGroup: World Bank income group country associated . admUnitCount: number administrative units boundary. meanVertices: mean number vertices defining boundaries administrative unit layer. minVertices: minimum number vertices defining boundary. maxVertices: maximum number vertices defining boundary. minPerimeterLengthKM: minimum perimeter length administrative unit layer, measured kilometers based World Equidistant Cylindrical projection. meanPerimeterLengthKM: mean perimeter length administrative unit layer, measured kilometers based World Equidistant Cylindrical projection. maxPerimeterLengthKM: maximum perimeter length administrative unit layer, measured kilometers based World Equidistant Cylindrical projection. meanAreaSqKM: mean area administrative units layer, measured square kilometers based EASE-GRID 2 projection. minAreaSqKM: minimum area administrative unit layer, measured square kilometers based EASE-GRID 2 projection. maxAreaSqKM: maximum area administrative unit layer, measured square kilometers based EASE-GRID 2 projection. staticDownloadLink: static download link aggregate ZIP file containing boundary information. gjDownloadURL: static download link GeoJSON. tjDownloadURL: static download link TopoJSON. imagePreview: static download link automatically rendered PNG image layer. simplifiedGeometryGeoJSON: static download link simplified GeoJSON.

title: Retrieve boundary metadata from geoBoundaries — gb_get_metadata
code: # Get boundary metadata for ADM4.  library(dplyr)  gb_get_metadata(adm_lvl = "ADM4") |>   glimpse() #> Rows: 21 #> Columns: 32 #> $ boundaryID                <chr> "AUT-ADM4-71187352", "BEL-ADM4-45533444", "B… #> $ boundaryName              <chr> "Austria", "Belgium", "Bangladesh", "Czechia… #> $ boundaryISO               <chr> "AUT", "BEL", "BGD", "CZE", "FJI", "FRA", "G… #> $ boundaryYearRepresented   <chr> "2017", "2018", "2020", "2017", "2007", "201… #> $ boundaryType              <chr> "ADM4", "ADM4", "ADM4", "ADM4", "ADM4", "ADM… #> $ boundaryCanonical         <chr> "Unknown", "Unknown", "Union Councils / Muni… #> $ boundarySource            <chr> "Federal Office for Metrology and Survey, Au… #> $ boundaryLicense           <chr> "Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 2.0… #> $ licenseDetail             <chr> NA, "Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 Intern… #> $ licenseSource             <chr> "twitter.com/WMgeoLab/status/125348789431395… #> $ boundarySourceURL         <chr> "www.bev.gv.at/portal/page?_pageid=713,26393… #> $ sourceDataUpdateDate      <dttm> 2023-01-19 07:31:04, 2023-07-10 13:37:13, 2… #> $ buildDate                 <date> 2023-12-12, 2023-12-12, 2023-12-12, 2023-12… #> $ Continent                 <chr> "Europe", "Europe", "Asia", "Europe", "Ocean… #> $ `UNSDG-region`            <chr> "Europe and Northern America", "Europe and N… #> $ `UNSDG-subregion`         <chr> "Western Europe", "Western Europe", "Souther… #> $ worldBankIncomeGroup      <chr> "High-income Countries", "High-income Countr… #> $ admUnitCount 53, 632, 11, 4, 38, 31, 4,… #> $ maxVertices               <dbl> 52740, 1084, 9182, 4028, 847, 15387, 10383, … #> $ meanPerimeterLengthKM     <dbl> 20.856955, 45.147426, 33.020823, 17.697541, … #> $ minPerimeterLengthKM      <dbl> 0.5618598, 6.5279918, 1.3478627, 0.3701208, … #> $ maxPerimeterLengthKM      <dbl> 152.20420, 115.80431, 1004.40607, 80.51895, … #> $ meanAreaSqKM              <dbl> 10.693872, 52.056224, 27.103105, 6.025353, 1… #> $ minAreaSqKM               <dbl> 0.004377737, 1.156515012, 0.026838648, 0.002… #> $ maxAreaSqKM               <dbl> 467.16996, 215.52080, 1230.96466, 87.32215, … #> $ staticDownloadLink        <chr> "https://github.com/wmgeolab/geoBoundaries/r… #> $ gjDownloadURL             <chr> "https://github.com/wmgeolab/geoBoundaries/r… #> $ tjDownloadURL title: Download global composite boundaries from geoBoundaries — gb_get_world
text: Comprehensive Global Administrative Zones (CGAZ) global composites administrative boundaries. Compared individual country boundaries, global composite boundaries use extensive simplification file sizes small enough desktop software. remove disputed areas, replace polygons following United States Department State definitions fill gaps borders. Follow citation use information included downloaded CGAZ archive. CGAZ figures derived relicensed package's MIT license.

what: Set the geobounds cache directory — gb_set_cache_dir
title: Set the geobounds cache directory — gb_set_cache_dir
text: Sets active cache directory optionally saves future sessions. Use gb_detect_cache_dir() find active cache directory.

title: Set the geobounds cache directory — gb_set_cache_dir
code: gb_set_cache_dir(   cache_dir = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   install = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Set the geobounds cache directory — gb_set_cache_dir
text: cache_dir path cache directory. NULL, function stores cached archives temporary directory. See base::tempdir(). overwrite logical value. TRUE, replace cache directory already saved configuration file. install logical value. TRUE, save cache directory use future sessions. Defaults FALSE. cache_dir NULL, parameter set FALSE automatically. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppress informational messages.

title: Set the geobounds cache directory — gb_set_cache_dir
text: invisible character scalar containing path cache directory.

title: Set the geobounds cache directory — gb_set_cache_dir
text: default, cache_dir set, geobounds uses directory inside base::tempdir(). Cached archives directory removed R session ends. reuse cache directory across R sessions, use gb_set_cache_dir(cache_dir = "/path/", install = TRUE). saves directory configuration file tools::R_user_dir("geobounds", "config").

title: Set the geobounds cache directory — gb_set_cache_dir
text: occasional use, use default temporary cache directory. Set cache directory current session gb_set_cache_dir(cache_dir = "/path/"). Save persistent cache directory future R sessions gb_set_cache_dir(cache_dir = "/path/", install = TRUE). Set cache directory individual download cache_dir argument. See gb_get().

title: Set the geobounds cache directory — gb_set_cache_dir
code: # Caution: this may modify your current state.  # \dontrun{ my_cache <- gb_detect_cache_dir() #> ℹ /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds  # Set an example cache directory. ex <- file.path(tempdir(), "example", "cachenew") gb_set_cache_dir(ex) #> ✔ geobounds cache directory is /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/example/cachenew. #> ℹ To use this cache directory in future sessions, call `gb_set_cache_dir()` with `install = TRUE`.  gb_detect_cache_dir() #> ℹ /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/example/cachenew #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/example/cachenew"  # Restore the initial cache. gb_set_cache_dir(my_cache) #> ✔ geobounds cache directory is /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds. #> ℹ To use this cache directory in future sessions, call `gb_set_cache_dir()` with `install = TRUE`. identical(my_cache, gb_detect_cache_dir()) #> ℹ /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds #> [1] TRUE # }  gb_detect_cache_dir() #> ℹ /tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpWYKfmL/geobounds"

what: geobounds: Download Administrative Boundary Data from 'geoBoundaries' — geobounds-package

text: Provides tools download individual country boundaries global composite boundaries 'geoBoundaries' https://www.geoboundaries.org/ across multiple administrative ('ADM') levels. Returns boundaries 'sf' objects mapping spatial analysis. Runfola et al. (2020) doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0231866  describe underlying database.

text: Runfola et al. (2020) "geoBoundaries: global database political administrative boundaries." PLOS ONE, 15(4), 1–9. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0231866 .

title: geobounds: Download Administrative Boundary Data from 'geoBoundaries' — geobounds-package
text: Maintainer: Diego Hernangómez diego.hernangomezherrero@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Diego Hernangómez diego.hernangomezherrero@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] contributors: William Mary geoLab (ROR) (geoBoundaries project) [copyright holder]

title: geobounds 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-08 Changed package software license CC 4.0 MIT clarified downloaded boundaries included figures retain licenses attribution requirements geoBoundaries original sources. Documentation now distinguishes varying gbOpen licenses, UN OCHA terms non-commercial restriction UN SALB boundaries. Downloads gbAuthoritative now display licensing notice. Made cache-related tests safer isolating GEOBOUNDS_CACHE_DIR withr::local_envvar() using test-owned temporary cache directories withr::local_tempdir().

title: geobounds 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-29 Improved documentation user-facing messages align terminology across roxygen2 narrative documentation clarify package output.

title: geobounds 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-24 Migrated package documentation Quarto. Updated package documentation.

title: geobounds 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-11 First CRAN release.

title: geobounds 0.1.0
text: Functions downloading boundaries renamed follow convention object_verb() (see https://devguide.ropensci.org/pkg_building.html): get_gb() -> gb_get(). ?get_gb_adm family -> ?gb_get_adm family. get_gb_cgaz() -> gb_get_world(). gb_get_cgaz() also available backward compatibility alias gb_get_world(). get_gb_meta() -> gb_get_metadata(). Additionally, metadata argument removed. Use gb_get_metadata() instead.

title: geobounds 0.1.0
text: Added DOI. download functions now use .zip archives containing .shp files instead .geojson .gpkg files, removing geojsonsf dependency Imports. download functions now return MULTIPOLYGON geometries. download functions now return NULL without error warning country file unavailable. download functions now retry transient request failures httr2::req_retry(). Cached files previous package versions longer reused source archive format changed. Country matching now improves detection Antarctica Kosovo. gb_get*() functions now allow mixed country argument types, gb_get(country = c("Germany", "USA")). gb_get_adm5() added. gb_get_max_adm_lvl() added. gb_get_world() now retrieves latest boundaries available repository https://github.com/wmgeolab/geoBoundaries/tree/main/releaseData.

title: geobounds 0.0.1
text: Initial release.
